title: China-to-Jordan BESS Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: 50 Hz Grid Basis and Jordan Site Settings
rows.target_requirement: Jordan and China both use a 50 Hz power-system basis, which helps PCS, transformer, and auxiliary-load screening. The frequency match does not remove the need for Jordan-specific voltage, protection, power-quality, grounding, communications, metering, dispatch, and commissioning settings at the actual point of connection.
target_standards: NEPCO or distribution-company interconnection requirements
Project-specific protection, power-quality, metering, and commissioning studies
EMRC regulatory or licensing conditions where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China 50 Hz PCS factory settings and GB/T 36558-2023 system evidence are useful starting points. They should be revalidated for Jordan voltage class, transformer taps, local protection settings, utility frequency and voltage ride-through windows, harmonics, grounding, ambient conditions, and communications.
rows.gap: Produce Jordan-specific relay settings, grid model, power-quality study, short-circuit study, SCADA protocol, commissioning plan, and site acceptance records instead of relying only on China factory settings.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected BESS where utility interconnection, EMRC conditions, grid-code, EPC, owner, insurer, lender, or commissioning approval applies; details are project and connection-point specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or contract-specific for China grid-connected projects through grid-company technical review, connection agreement, and commissioning acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] The 50 Hz match is helpful engineering context, not an interconnection approval. Jordan site settings and utility commissioning remain separate requirements.

rows.requirement: China GB 44240-2024 / GB/T 36276-2023 Crosswalk to Jordan Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Jordan reviewers may accept or request IEC-numbered evidence, Jordan standards, contract specifications, or project-specific technical files. China GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 should therefore be presented as mapped technical evidence, not as automatic substitutes for Jordan, IEC, EMRC, NEPCO, customs, or project requirements.
target_standards: JSMO standards and conformity route where applicable
IEC 62933 series
EMRC / NEPCO project requirements where grid-connected
rows.china_equivalent: GB 44240-2024 is the China mandatory safety standard for covered electric-energy-storage lithium-ion cells and batteries, while GB/T 36276-2023 is a common China recommended standard for lithium-ion batteries for electric energy storage. GB/T 36558-2023 is commonly used for electrochemical energy-storage system safety evidence.
rows.gap: Prepare a clause-by-clause crosswalk that identifies identical, partially covered, not covered, and not applicable clauses against IEC 62619 and the relevant IEC 62933 parts. Attach English translations, lab accreditation evidence, sample traceability, firmware and BOM version control, and explicit acceptance records from the Jordan reviewer.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific or mandatory only when Jordan standards, JSMO route, EMRC / NEPCO review, customs, tender, owner, insurer, lender, or contract accepts or requires the mapped evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory for covered China domestic products; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are recommended unless incorporated by regulation, project, or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB evidence can reduce retesting and review effort only when mapped and accepted. It should not be marketed as a standalone Jordan approval.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation, National Public Service Platform for Standards Information

rows.target_requirement: Jordan BESS fire safety is normally a project and site review rather than a single public product mark. Project owners, civil-defense or local authorities, insurers, EPCs, and lenders may request container layout, separation distances, fire detection and suppression design, ventilation, gas detection where relevant, thermal-runaway mitigation, emergency shutdown, access routes, spill and water-runoff controls, and local-language emergency procedures.
target_standards: Jordan local civil-defense, building, fire, and site-permit review where applicable
Project-specific fire-safety specification
IEC 62933 system-safety evidence where requested
rows.china_equivalent: China commonly relies on GB 44240-2024, GB/T 36276-2023, GB/T 36558-2023, container fire-suppression design, and project fire-safety review for stationary energy storage. These documents may support technical due diligence but do not replace Jordan site, insurer, civil-defense, or owner acceptance.
rows.gap: Build a site-specific fire-safety dossier rather than relying only on cell or rack certificates. Include thermal-runaway test evidence, enclosure drawings, fire-detection logic, suppression design, HVAC and ventilation basis, emergency shutdown, signage, maintenance procedures, incident-response plan, and Arabic or English operating instructions requested by the Jordan project.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where Jordan civil-defense, building, fire, site permit, project owner, insurer, lender, EPC, or utility review requires fire-safety approval or evidence; details are project and site specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or project-specific depending on Chinese project approval, local fire review, contract, and stationary energy-storage standard scope

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Battery safety evidence alone does not close the Jordan BESS fire-safety gap. Site fire design and local acceptance should be handled before shipment or commissioning.

rows.requirement: EMRC / NEPCO Storage and Grid-Connection Review
rows.target_requirement: A grid-connected BESS in Jordan should be treated as a project-specific electricity regulatory and utility review. EMRC is the energy regulator, while NEPCO is the national transmission and system operator interface. Depending on project model and connection point, storage may need licensing or approval, interconnection studies, protection and metering review, dispatch or operating conditions, communications, commissioning tests, and contract-specific storage controls.
target_standards: EMRC electricity licensing, renewable-energy, and regulatory instructions where applicable
NEPCO grid-connection and operating requirements
Project-specific PPA, wheeling, self-generation, tender, or connection agreement
rows.china_equivalent: China-side grid files commonly include GB/T 36558-2023 system evidence, PCS factory settings, protection studies, and China grid-company commissioning records. These materials can support engineering review but do not replace EMRC approval or NEPCO connection acceptance.
rows.gap: Prepare a Jordan-specific grid package covering point of connection, voltage class, short-circuit duty, protection coordination, anti-islanding, active and reactive power control, ramp-rate limits, harmonics, grounding, metering, SCADA / telemetry, dispatch instructions, commissioning tests, and storage operating modes.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected BESS where EMRC licensing or approval, NEPCO interconnection, distribution-company approval, tender, PPA, owner, insurer, or lender requires review; details are project-specific
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory or contract-specific for China grid-connected BESS through grid-company technical review, connection agreement, and commissioning acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China grid evidence is not EMRC or NEPCO acceptance. Treat Jordan BESS interconnection as a project-specific regulator, utility, and contract review.

source.publisher: Energy and Minerals Regulatory Commission (EMRC), Jordan

rows.requirement: JSMO Scope Screening and IEC 62619 / IEC 62933 Evidence
rows.target_requirement: Jordan's JSMO is the national standards body and provides standards search, conformity, and pre-verification service entry points. For lithium BESS, public sources should be treated as a scope-screening gate rather than proof of one universal whole-system certificate. Project owners, lenders, insurers, EPCs, or reviewers may request IEC 62619 safety evidence for industrial lithium cells and batteries and IEC 62933 evidence for electrical energy storage systems.
target_standards: Jordan Standards and Metrology Organization standards and conformity routes
IEC 62933 series
IEC 62933-5-2 where system-safety evidence is requested
rows.china_equivalent: China BESS files commonly include GB 44240-2024 and GB/T 36276-2023 battery evidence, plus GB/T 36558-2023 system evidence. These documents can support technical due diligence but do not by themselves prove JSMO acceptance, IEC-numbered certification, or project acceptance in Jordan.
rows.gap: Confirm the exact Jordan route by HS code, product configuration, bill of materials, contract, and project specification. Prepare English technical files, accredited-lab reports, certificates where available, and a clause matrix showing where China GB / GB/T evidence maps to IEC 62619 and the relevant IEC 62933 parts.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where a Jordan standard, technical regulation, customs route, conformity route, tender, contract, utility, insurer, lender, or authority calls up the exact product, component, standard, or evidence package
mandatory_status.cn: GB 44240-2024 is mandatory for covered China domestic electric-energy-storage lithium-ion cells and batteries; GB/T 36276-2023 and GB/T 36558-2023 are recommended unless incorporated by regulation, project, or contract

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat JSMO as a scope-confirmation and standards-evidence gate. Do not assume China GB reports are accepted in Jordan without an explicit JSMO, project, or reviewer decision.

rows.target_requirement: Lithium cells and batteries shipped to Jordan are subject to dangerous-goods transport expectations. A BESS shipment normally needs UN 38.3 test evidence for cells and batteries, test summaries, classification, packing, marks and labels, and mode-specific air, sea, road, or multimodal documents before the product reaches the Jordan site.
target_standards: UN Manual of Tests and Criteria, Part III, Sub-section 38.3
UN Model Regulations dangerous-goods classification for lithium batteries
Applicable ICAO / IATA, IMDG, road, customs, carrier, and port requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China export files often include UN 38.3 reports, MSDS / SDS, transport appraisal certificates, and lithium-battery marks. These are useful only if they match the exact cell, module, battery, state of charge, packaging, and shipment mode used for Jordan.
china_standards: UN 38.3 test report and test summary
SDS / MSDS for lithium battery shipment
rows.gap: Match the transport file to the final shipment configuration. Re-check large containerized BESS units, spare batteries, replacement modules, damaged or prototype batteries, and mixed consignments because transport classification can change even when product safety evidence is unchanged.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for lithium batteries transported as dangerous goods where UN 38.3, carrier, port, customs, or modal transport rules apply
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for China export transport where lithium-battery dangerous-goods transport rules, carrier rules, or customs documentation require UN 38.3 and related evidence

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] UN 38.3 is a shipment gate, not a Jordan market certificate. Verify the exact BESS logistics route before booking or customs filing.

source.publisher: United Nations Economic Commission for Europe (UNECE)
